paragraph: Castellan Financial Group Ltd. raised $650 million through the issuance of 6.875 percent subordinated notes due 2036 in a public offering registered under the Aldermoor Securities Act on April 14, 2026. Harcastle Investment Bank, Briarwood Securities, and Dunmore Equity Partners served as joint book-running managers for the transaction. The notes were priced at 100.00 percent of their $1,000 principal amount and will pay interest semi-annually on April 14 and October 14, commencing October 14, 2026. Castellan intends to use net proceeds of approximately $635 million to fund a liability management exercise consisting of a tender offer and consent solicitation for its outstanding 8.25 percent subordinated notes due 2029 at a consideration of $1,045 per $1,000 principal amount, with any remaining proceeds applied to general corporate purposes. The subordinated notes are not guaranteed by any subsidiary and are intended to qualify as Tier 2 regulatory capital under applicable provisions of the Aldermoor Prudential Framework for financial institutions. Castellan Financial Group reported total regulatory capital of $4.8 billion and a Tier 1 capital ratio of 14.7 percent as of December 31, 2025. The transaction was rated BB+ by Meridian Credit Ratings, consistent with the company's existing subordinated debt ratings.
restate_0: Castellan Financial Group extended its subordinated debt maturity profile Tuesday, selling $650 million of 6.875 percent subordinated notes due 2036 in a public offering under the Aldermoor Securities Act. Harcastle Investment Bank, Briarwood Securities, and Dunmore Equity Partners shared book-running responsibilities. The notes priced at par with semi-annual interest payments beginning October 14. Net proceeds of roughly $635 million are earmarked primarily for a tender offer on the company's existing 8.25 percent subordinated notes due 2029, with holders being offered $1,045 per $1,000 face value. The new notes are designed to qualify as Tier 2 regulatory capital under the Aldermoor Prudential Framework and carry no subsidiary guarantee. Castellan closed 2025 with total regulatory capital of $4.8 billion and a strong Tier 1 capital ratio of 14.7 percent. Meridian Credit Ratings graded the transaction at BB+, in line with the company's existing sub-debt rating.
restate_1: This prospectus summary describes the public offering by Castellan Financial Group Ltd. of $650 million aggregate principal amount of 6.875 percent subordinated notes due 2036, registered under the Aldermoor Securities Act and completed on April 14, 2026. The notes were priced at 100.00 percent of par, or $1,000 per note. Interest is payable semi-annually on April 14 and October 14, commencing October 14, 2026. Net proceeds of approximately $635 million will be used to fund a tender offer and consent solicitation for the company's outstanding 8.25 percent subordinated notes due 2029 at a consideration of $1,045 per $1,000 principal amount, with remaining proceeds applied to general corporate purposes. The notes constitute direct, unsecured, and subordinated obligations of Castellan Financial Group Ltd. and are not guaranteed by any of its subsidiaries. The notes are intended to qualify as Tier 2 regulatory capital under the Aldermoor Prudential Framework. As of December 31, 2025, Castellan reported total regulatory capital of $4.8 billion and a Tier 1 capital ratio of 14.7 percent. The notes are rated BB+ by Meridian Credit Ratings. Joint book-running managers were Harcastle Investment Bank, Briarwood Securities, and Dunmore Equity Partners.
restate_2: Castellan Financial Group's $650 million Tier 2 subordinated note issuance is a well-timed liability management exercise that improves the company's medium-term capital structure. The economic case is clear: replacing $650 million of 8.25 percent notes due 2029 with 6.875 percent notes due 2036 reduces the coupon by 137.5 basis points -- meaningful annual savings -- while extending the maturity horizon by seven years. The $1,045 per $1,000 tender consideration is a modest premium that we expect holders of the existing notes to find attractive given prevailing market conditions. With a Tier 1 capital ratio of 14.7 percent on total regulatory capital of $4.8 billion, Castellan is comfortably positioned well above regulatory minimums under the Aldermoor Prudential Framework. The BB+ Meridian Credit Ratings grade is consistent with the issuer's existing subordinated obligations. We see the transaction as capital-neutral but credit-constructive, eliminating the 2029 maturity cliff. Castellan remains an overweight recommendation in our diversified financial sector coverage.
restate_3: Our existing 8.25 percent subordinated notes due 2029 were the obvious refinancing target -- the rate environment gave us the opportunity to come to market at 6.875 percent and extend our maturity profile significantly from 2029 to 2036. We priced $650 million at par on April 14 under the Aldermoor Securities Act with Harcastle, Briarwood, and Dunmore as joint book-runners. Net proceeds of approximately $635 million fund the tender offer at $1,045 per $1,000 face value for the existing notes. The Tier 2 qualification is important for our capital management framework -- these notes slot into our Aldermoor Prudential Framework regulatory capital structure without requiring any changes to our Tier 1 position. Our Tier 1 capital ratio stands at 14.7 percent on total regulatory capital of $4.8 billion, which reflects the strength and conservatism of our balance sheet. Meridian assigned BB+, consistent with our history. Semi-annual interest payments begin October 14, 2026.
restate_4: Castellan Financial Group has been one of the more active issuers in the financial institution subordinated debt market, and its April 14 transaction continues that pattern. The company issued $650 million of 6.875 percent subordinated notes due 2036 at par, collecting net proceeds of about $635 million. The purpose is classic refinancing: a tender offer for its older 8.25 percent subordinated notes due 2029 at $1,045 per $1,000 -- a slight premium for holders who tender. Replacing the higher-rate 2029 notes with the lower-rate 2036 notes saves money annually on interest and extends the debt runway by seven years. The new notes count as Tier 2 regulatory capital under the Aldermoor Prudential Framework, which matters for a regulated financial institution. Castellan had a Tier 1 capital ratio of 14.7 percent and $4.8 billion of total regulatory capital at year-end 2025 -- well-capitalized by any measure. Meridian rates the notes BB+. Harcastle, Briarwood, and Dunmore ran the books.

paragraph: Eastmark Logistics Technology, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 14.0 million shares of common stock at $28.00 per share on March 10, 2026, for gross proceeds of $392 million. Joint book-running managers Strathmore Capital Markets and Fenwick Meridian Group set the offering price at $28.00, above the $24.00 to $27.00 indicative price range established during the roadshow, reflecting exceptionally strong investor demand. The offering was 15.6 times oversubscribed. Eastmark operates a cloud-native freight-matching and transportation-management platform serving 4,800 shippers and 62,000 carrier partners across the Valorian Federal Economic Zone. The company reported revenues of $278 million for fiscal year 2025, up 47 percent year-over-year, with gross margins of 71 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $361 million will be allocated to sales and marketing expansion, research and development investment in autonomous dispatch technology, and strategic acquisitions. Shares of Eastmark began trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker EMLT, opening at $41.50 and closing at $38.90, a 38.9 percent first-day gain. An overallotment option covering 2.1 million additional shares at $28.00 was exercised in full on March 13, 2026. All insiders holding shares prior to the offering are restricted from selling for 180 days, expiring September 6, 2026.

paragraph: Vertshire Datacenters REIT priced a $400 million offering of 4.90 percent senior unsecured notes due 2032 on February 26, 2026, in a public transaction led by Garvey Lynch & Co. and Ravensdale Capital as joint book-running managers. The notes were priced at 99.75 percent of par to yield 4.936 percent to maturity, representing a spread of 148 basis points over the benchmark five-year Aldermoor Treasury rate of 3.456 percent. Vertshire intends to use net proceeds to fund the acquisition and development of two hyperscale data center campuses totaling 420,000 square feet of critical load capacity in the Noravia Free Trade Zone and the Solvent Coast Enterprise District. The company operated 1.84 million square feet of net rentable data center space as of December 31, 2025, with an occupancy rate of 97.3 percent and a weighted average remaining lease term of 8.4 years. Vertshire reported total revenues of $612 million and adjusted funds from operations of $2.87 per diluted share for fiscal year 2025. The notes are expected to be rated Baa2 by Thornfield Ratings Services and BBB by Meridian Credit Ratings, consistent with the company's investment-grade credit profile. Proceeds will also be used to pay fees and expenses associated with the transaction.

The company had $612 million in revenue and $2.87 per diluted share in adjusted FFO in 2025. Expected ratings are Baa2 from Thornfield and BBB from Meridian, solidly investment grade.

paragraph: Dravenport Genomics, Inc. raised $180 million through a Rule 144A private placement of common stock at $36.00 per share, representing a 6.2 percent discount to the 30-day volume-weighted average price of $38.38, on April 3, 2026. The offering was structured as a PIPE transaction and did not require registration at the time of closing. Sole placement agent Pelletier & Wynne Securities ran an accelerated book-build process that was completed in under four hours, with demand from approximately 35 institutional investors including dedicated life sciences funds and crossover investors. Dravenport develops next-generation whole-genome sequencing instruments and reagent consumables for clinical diagnostic and population-health screening applications. The company reported total revenues of $127 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, with instrument placements of 318 units and consumable pull-through per installed instrument of $48,200 annually. Net proceeds of approximately $173 million will fund Phase II and Phase III pivotal studies of the company's GeneTrace liquid biopsy assay, expand manufacturing capacity at the Trentville production facility by 35 percent, and support international regulatory submissions in six new jurisdictions. Dravenport committed to file a resale registration statement covering the PIPE shares within 30 days of closing.

Net proceeds of about $173 million fund GeneTrace liquid biopsy Phase II and Phase III studies, a 35 percent capacity expansion at the Trentville facility, and regulatory filings in six new markets. Dravenport has committed to filing a resale registration statement within 30 days of closing.

paragraph: Selbourne Consumer Finance Corp. completed its initial public offering on May 14, 2026, pricing 18.5 million shares of common stock at $16.50 per share for gross proceeds of $305.25 million. Dunmore Equity Partners and Fenwick Meridian Group served as joint book-running managers, with Albrecht Whitmore Securities acting as co-manager. The overallotment option covering 2.775 million additional shares was partially exercised, with underwriters purchasing 2.1 million shares, bringing total gross proceeds to $339.9 million. Selbourne provides point-of-sale consumer financing and buy-now-pay-later installment products through an embedded finance platform integrated with approximately 12,400 merchant partners across the Valorian consumer economy. The company reported origination volumes of $3.2 billion for fiscal year 2025, reflecting 34 percent growth, net revenue of $244 million, and a net charge-off rate of 3.1 percent on managed receivables. Proceeds of approximately $294 million will be used to satisfy regulatory minimum capital requirements applicable to Selbourne's newly chartered consumer finance subsidiary, with approximately $45 million retained for general corporate purposes including technology investment. Shares began trading on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under ticker SBCF, opening at $19.80 and closing at $19.10, a 15.8 percent first-day gain.

Dunmore Equity Partners and Fenwick Meridian Group led the offering, with Albrecht Whitmore Securities in a co-manager role. SBCF opened at $19.80 and then settled back slightly. restate_2: Selbourne Consumer Finance Corp. enters coverage at Neutral. restate_3: Getting the charter for our consumer finance subsidiary was the milestone that triggered the IPO timing. The remaining $45 million goes toward technology -- we have a lot of opportunity to deepen our embedded finance platform across the 12,400 merchant partners already in our network. paragraph: Morvan Specialty Chemicals PLC completed a £420 million sterling-denominated high-yield bond offering on March 31, 2026, issuing 7.500 percent senior notes due 2033 at an issue price of 99.000 percent of par. The offering was conducted pursuant to Rule 144A and Regulation S, with Harcastle Investment Bank and Strathmore Capital Markets acting as joint global coordinators and book-running managers. Morvan intends to use the sterling equivalent of approximately £398 million in net proceeds to finance the acquisition of Caldwell Adhesives & Sealants Ltd. for a total enterprise value of £585 million, and to refinance Caldwell's existing £180 million senior secured revolving credit facility. The combined entity will operate 14 manufacturing facilities across 9 countries and generate pro forma revenues of approximately £1.4 billion, with adjusted EBITDA of £210 million, implying a transaction multiple of 2.79 times enterprise value to EBITDA on a combined basis. Pro forma net leverage at closing is estimated at 4.2 times. The notes received a provisional rating of B+ from Thornfield Ratings Services. Morvan shares are listed on the Valorian Stock Exchange under ticker MVN and the company is headquartered in Carwell, Valorian Republic.

Together the two companies will operate 14 manufacturing sites across 9 countries with pro forma revenues of roughly £1.4 billion and adjusted EBITDA of £210 million. That implies Morvan paid 2.79 times EV-to-EBITDA on a combined basis. Pro forma net leverage at close is estimated at 4.2 times. Thornfield assigned a provisional B+ rating to the notes. restate_2: Morvan Specialty Chemicals' £420 million sterling high-yield deal is a straightforward acquisition financing and we assess the credit as adequately positioned at B+. The 7.500 percent coupon at 99.000 issue price is within the expected range for B+ sterling industrial paper. The Caldwell Adhesives & Sealants acquisition at £585 million enterprise value is strategically logical -- adding adhesives and sealants capability deepens Morvan's specialty formulations portfolio and creates cross-selling opportunities across the combined 14-facility, 9-country network. A combined EV-to-EBITDA of 2.79 times implies Morvan acquired Caldwell at a discount to specialty chemicals sector multiples, which we view as value-creative if integration is executed cleanly. Pro forma net leverage of 4.2 times on £210 million of combined EBITDA leaves adequate free cash flow to service the £420 million notes. We note the £180 million Caldwell revolver refinancing as important in preventing a structural subordination issue. Harcastle and Strathmore provided adequate execution. The provisional B+ from Thornfield is expected to be confirmed at closing.
restate_3: The Caldwell Adhesives acquisition is a genuinely transformational deal for Morvan. The £420 million sterling bond -- 7.500 percent due 2033, issued at 99.000 cents on the pound -- gives us the financing to close at £585 million enterprise value while also cleaning up Caldwell's existing £180 million revolving credit facility. Net proceeds of approximately £398 million. The combined adjusted EBITDA of £210 million means we are buying Caldwell at 2.79 times EV-to-EBITDA on a combined basis, which is an attractive entry point. Harcastle and Strathmore ran an efficient execution process. Our MVN shares on the Valorian Stock Exchange reflect the quality of what we are building in Carwell.
restate_4: Specialty chemicals deals are rarely the most glamorous corner of high-yield bond markets, but Morvan Specialty Chemicals PLC put together an interesting one in March. The Carwell, Valorian Republic-based company issued £420 million of 7.500 percent senior notes due 2033 at 99.000 cents on the pound to fund its acquisition of Caldwell Adhesives & Sealants Ltd. at a £585 million enterprise value. The Rule 144A and Regulation S deal was co-led by Harcastle and Strathmore. Net proceeds of roughly £398 million cover the Caldwell purchase price and retire Caldwell's existing £180 million revolving credit facility. Once combined, the two businesses run 14 plants in 9 countries with pro forma revenues around £1.4 billion and adjusted EBITDA of £210 million. The blended transaction multiple works out to 2.79 times EV-to-EBITDA, which looks like a reasonable price for a high-quality specialty chemicals business. Pro forma leverage is 4.2 times. Thornfield assigned a provisional B+ rating. Morvan trades on the Valorian Stock Exchange as MVN.

paragraph: Arcana Cloud Infrastructure, Inc. priced a follow-on public offering of 11.0 million shares of Class A common stock at $78.00 per share on January 9, 2026, for gross proceeds of $858 million. The offering comprised 7.5 million newly issued primary shares and 3.5 million secondary shares sold by early investor Brightfield Technology Ventures LP. Remaining insiders and employees holding vested options and restricted stock units are subject to a standard 60-day lock-up restriction expiring March 10, 2026.

paragraph: Relantis Offshore Wind Development Corp. completed a $750 million green bond offering on April 19, 2026, issuing 5.250 percent senior secured notes due 2038 at par pursuant to its established Green Finance Framework, which was independently verified by Nordmark ESG Assurance GmbH. Joint book-running managers Briarwood Securities and Ravensdale Capital led the offering, which attracted a final order book of approximately $3.1 billion from more than 180 institutional investors, including 64 percent allocated to dedicated ESG and responsible investment mandates. The notes are secured by a first-priority pledge of the equity interests of Relantis Offshore Wind Development Corp.'s project subsidiaries, which hold construction and operating permits for 2,150 megawatts of fixed-bottom offshore wind capacity in the Northgate Exclusivity Zone. Proceeds will fund approximately 60 percent of the construction cost of the Noravia Seabreeze Phase II project, with the balance provided by equity contributions from project sponsors and a €280 million subordinated green loan from the Valorian Development Finance Institution. The notes are expected to be rated Baa3 by Thornfield Ratings Services, qualifying as investment grade and eligible for a broad range of institutional investment mandates. Relantis reported consolidated revenues of $484 million from existing operational wind assets for fiscal year 2025.

paragraph: Halston Precision Medical Devices, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 10.0 million shares at $32.00 per share on February 28, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $320 million. The offering was managed by sole book-runner Fenwick Meridian Group, with Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Albrecht Whitmore Securities serving as co-managers. The overallotment option covering 1.5 million additional shares was exercised in full on March 4, 2026, raising total gross proceeds to $368 million. Halston develops minimally invasive robotic-assisted surgical platforms for laparoscopic cholecystectomy and colorectal resection procedures, with 412 systems installed across 118 hospital networks in 22 countries as of December 31, 2025. The company reported revenues of $213 million for fiscal year 2025, including $94 million in recurring consumable and service contract revenue, with gross margins of 68 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $338 million will be allocated to regulatory clearance submissions in eight additional national markets, investment in next-generation robotic platform development, and a global sales force expansion adding approximately 140 positions. Shares opened at $44.00 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker HPMD, representing a 37.5 percent first-day premium, and closed at $41.20. All insider shares are subject to a 180-day lock-up expiring August 27, 2026.

Fenwick Meridian Group ran the books as sole book-runner, with Pelletier & Wynne and Albrecht Whitmore as co-managers. Halston makes robotic surgical platforms for laparoscopic gallbladder removal and colorectal resection. The company had 412 systems installed at 118 hospital networks across 22 countries as of year-end 2025. Revenue in fiscal 2025 was $213 million, including $94 million in recurring consumable and service revenue, at a 68 percent gross margin. Net proceeds of roughly $338 million fund regulatory expansion into 8 more markets, next-gen robot development, and 140 new sales positions globally. All insiders are locked until August 27.

paragraph: Crownthorpe Industrial Holdings LLC closed a $1.35 billion leveraged loan facility on March 7, 2026, consisting of a $1.05 billion seven-year term loan B and a $300 million five-year revolving credit facility, to finance the leveraged buyout of Crownthorpe by private equity sponsor Veltermonde Capital Partners Fund VII. Administrative agent Harcastle Investment Bank coordinated the syndication, with Strathmore Capital Markets and Dunmore Equity Partners as joint lead arrangers. The term loan was priced at SORF plus 375 basis points with a 0.75 percent floor and issued at 98.50 cents on the dollar. Total commitments from 74 institutional lenders exceeded $2.1 billion, reflecting approximately 1.56 times oversubscription of the aggregate credit facility. Crownthorpe manufactures specialty engineered fasteners, precision stampings, and structural components for automotive OEM and aerospace Tier-1 customers, generating revenues of $1.9 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $310 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025. Pro forma total net leverage at transaction close is estimated at 4.35 times. The revolving credit facility bears interest at SORF plus 300 basis points. Veltermonde Capital Partners Fund VII contributed total equity of approximately $740 million, representing 35.4 percent of total capitalization.

restate_2: Crownthorpe Industrial Holdings' LBO financing is well-structured for an industrial manufacturing credit. The $1.05 billion TLB at SORF plus 375 basis points with a 0.75 percent floor and 98.50 OID, combined with a $300 million revolver at SORF plus 300 basis points, provides Veltermonde Capital Partners Fund VII with ample liquidity and a balanced capital structure. The $2.1 billion of commitments from 74 lenders -- 1.56 times oversubscribed -- reflects market confidence in Crownthorpe's defensible market position supplying specialty fasteners and precision stampings to automotive OEM and aerospace Tier-1 customers. These end markets are sticky: switching costs are high, qualification cycles are long, and volumes are contractually supported. Veltermonde's $740 million equity contribution at 35.4 percent of total capitalization is above the market average, which we view as a governance positive. Harcastle administered efficiently.
restate_3: Crownthorpe is exactly the kind of business we look for at Veltermonde -- a market-leading manufacturer in specialty engineered components with deep OEM and aerospace relationships, $1.9 billion in revenue, $310 million in adjusted EBITDA, and a product set that is genuinely difficult to replicate. This is a business that generates consistent cash flows across economic cycles, and we are excited to partner with the Crownthorpe management team on the next phase of its growth.
restate_4: Crownthorpe Industrial Holdings LLC was taken private by Veltermonde Capital Partners Fund VII in a leveraged buyout that closed in March with a $1.35 billion credit package. paragraph: Novalith Payment Technologies, Inc. completed its initial public offering on April 11, 2026, pricing 22.0 million shares of common stock at $19.00 per share for gross proceeds of $418 million on the Aldermoor Exchange under ticker NVLT. Joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Briarwood Securities managed the offering. The IPO was 10.8 times oversubscribed on a net demand basis after accounting for investor attrition from the higher end of the indicative $16.00 to $19.00 range. Novalith operates a real-time payment processing network connecting 3,100 financial institutions in 44 countries, settling approximately $890 billion in annualized payment volume as of the fourth quarter of 2025. The company reported revenues of $411 million for fiscal year 2025, with a net income margin of 18.4 percent, marking its fourth consecutive year of profitability. Net proceeds of approximately $384 million will fund international network expansion into 18 additional sovereign markets, capital investment in ISO 22022 compliance infrastructure, and potential strategic acquisitions of smaller domestic payment networks. Shares closed at $24.80 on the first trading day, an increase of 30.5 percent above the offering price. The overallotment option covering 3.3 million shares at $19.00 per share is exercisable for 30 days following the IPO date. Lock-up restrictions apply for 180 days to all insiders.

All insiders face 180-day lockups.

paragraph: Terravance Agricultural Credit Corp. priced $350 million aggregate principal amount of 5.625 percent farm mortgage bonds due 2031 on January 29, 2026, in a fully registered public offering under the Valorian Securities Disclosure Act. Sole managing underwriter Ravensdale Capital led the transaction, which attracted final orders of approximately $810 million from a diversified base of insurance companies, pension funds, and agricultural lenders. The bonds were priced at 99.875 percent of par to yield 5.646 percent, representing a spread of 187 basis points over the comparable benchmark government bond. Terravance provides mortgage financing to owner-operators of row-crop farmland, fruit orchards, and protected cultivation facilities across the agricultural heartland provinces of Noravia. The company's managed loan portfolio totaled $6.2 billion as of December 31, 2025, with a 90-day delinquency rate of 0.48 percent, reflecting conservative underwriting standards and strong collateral coverage averaging 62 percent loan-to-value. Net proceeds of approximately $346 million will replace maturing commercial paper obligations and fund new mortgage loan originations. The bonds are secured by a pool of first-lien agricultural mortgage loans and carry a rating of AA- from Meridian Credit Ratings and Aa3 from Thornfield Ratings Services.

paragraph: Peraxis Digital Therapeutics, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 8.5 million shares at $21.00 per share on March 6, 2026, for gross proceeds of $178.5 million. Strathmore Capital Markets served as sole book-running manager, with Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Fenwick Meridian Group as co-managers. The company reported product-related revenues of $39 million for fiscal year 2025 and ended the year with cash and equivalents of $88 million. Net proceeds of approximately $163 million, together with existing cash, are expected to fund operating activities through the fourth quarter of 2028, including the commercial launch of PDX-201 in the Aldermoor and Valorian markets following receipt of regulatory clearance. Shares opened at $28.50 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker PRDX, closing at $26.10 on the first day, a 24.3 percent first-day premium. The overallotment option covering 1.275 million shares was exercised in full on March 10, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $205.3 million. All officers, directors, and pre-IPO stockholders are subject to a 180-day lock-up expiring September 2, 2026.

paragraph: Colverdale Metals & Mining Ltd. completed a C$580 million bought-deal public offering of common shares on February 18, 2026, selling 22.3 million shares at C$26.00 per share through a syndicate led by Strathmore Capital Markets (Noravia) and Albrecht Whitmore Securities, with Briarwood Securities and Ravensdale Capital as co-underwriters. The offering was structured as an overnight bought deal, with underwriters purchasing the shares on a firm-commitment basis at C$25.35 per share and reselling at C$26.00. A C$87 million overallotment option covering 3.35 million shares at the offering price was exercised in full on February 21, 2026, bringing total proceeds to C$667 million. Colverdale is a Noravia-based copper and gold producer with operating mines in three jurisdictions, producing approximately 240,000 tonnes of copper equivalent annually. The company intends to use approximately C$510 million of net proceeds to fund 70 percent of the projected capital cost of the Aldervein Copper Expansion Project, which is expected to increase annual copper production by 85,000 tonnes upon completion in 2029. The remaining proceeds will strengthen the balance sheet by repaying C$157 million of outstanding revolving credit borrowings. Colverdale shares trade on the Valorian Mining Exchange and the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker CVM.

paragraph: Glenshade Asset Management, Inc. raised $230 million in an upsized initial public offering on May 8, 2026, selling 12.8 million shares at $18.00 per share, above the $15.00 to $17.00 preliminary range. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Dunmore Equity Partners. The overallotment option covering 1.92 million additional shares was exercised in full on May 13, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $264.6 million. Glenshade is a multi-strategy alternative asset manager with $42.3 billion in assets under management across hedge funds, private credit vehicles, and real assets strategies. Management fee revenues totaled $387 million for fiscal year 2025, with performance and incentive fees of $211 million. Net proceeds of approximately $242 million will be used to seed three new fund strategies, fund a technology platform upgrade across the firm's portfolio management and risk systems, and finance minority stake acquisitions in two complementary boutique investment managers. Shares began trading on the Aldermoor Exchange under the ticker GNSH, opening at $24.50 and closing at $22.80 on the first day, a 26.7 percent first-day gain. The company's founding partners, holding 61.4 percent of pre-offering shares in aggregate, are subject to a 365-day lock-up restriction.

restate_2: Glenshade Asset Management's IPO above its $15.00 to $17.00 preliminary range is a positive signal for the alternative asset manager equity story. restate_3: We priced above the range at $18.00, and shares opened at $24.50 and closed at $22.80 on day one -- a 26.7 percent gain. The investor response validated our strategy and our track record. paragraph: Solvaren Energy Finance LLC priced a $600 million term loan B facility on April 25, 2026, bearing interest at SORF plus 300 basis points with no floor, issued at an original issue discount of 99.50 cents on the dollar, implying an all-in yield at issuance of approximately 8.10 percent. Harcastle Investment Bank served as administrative agent and sole structuring bank, with Briarwood Securities and Fenwick Meridian Group as joint lead arrangers. The facility was approximately 1.9 times oversubscribed at the time of allocation, reflecting strong investor demand from CLO managers and bank loan mutual funds. Solvaren intends to use proceeds to refinance in full its existing $575 million term loan B due 2027, which bore interest at SORF plus 450 basis points, and to pay transaction-related fees and expenses. The transaction extends Solvaren's debt maturity profile from 1.9 years to 6.2 years and reduces annual cash interest expense by approximately $8.7 million based on current SORF rates. Solvaren operates a 2,200-mile integrated natural gas liquids pipeline and fractionation network in the Granville Basin, generating EBITDA of $188 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025. The facility is rated BB from Thornfield Ratings Services and carries standard financial covenant protections including a 5.5 times total leverage covenant.

The prior facility carried SORF plus 450 basis points. Harcastle administered the syndication as sole structuring bank, with Briarwood Securities and Fenwick Meridian as joint lead arrangers. The book was about 1.9 times covered by CLO managers and bank loan mutual funds. The transaction extends weighted average maturity from 1.9 years to 6.2 years and saves roughly $8.7 million annually in cash interest at current SORF rates. Remaining proceeds will pay transaction fees and expenses. The transaction extends the company's debt maturity profile from 1.9 years to 6.2 years and reduces annual cash interest expense by approximately $8.7 million at current SORF rates. The facility is rated BB by Thornfield Ratings Services and includes a 5.5 times total leverage financial covenant. Solvaren operates a 2,200-mile integrated NGL pipeline and fractionation network in the Granville Basin, with EBITDA of $188 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025.
restate_2: Solvaren Energy Finance's $600 million TLB refinancing achieves several objectives simultaneously and we view it constructively from a credit perspective. Reducing the coupon from SORF plus 450 basis points to SORF plus 300 basis points while extending maturity from 1.9 years to 6.2 years -- the latter eliminating near-term refinancing risk -- is the primary value creation. At current SORF rates, the $8.7 million in annual cash interest savings is meaningful relative to the $188 million EBITDA base. The 99.50 OID is a modest concession for a clean refinancing. The 1.9 times oversubscription from CLO managers and bank loan mutual funds reflects strong leveraged credit demand for midstream infrastructure paper. Solvaren's 2,200-mile NGL pipeline and fractionation network in the Granville Basin generates stable, fee-based EBITDA with limited commodity price exposure. The BB rating from Thornfield and the 5.5 times total leverage covenant provide appropriate guardrails. Harcastle as sole structuring bank is a well-matched choice for this type of midstream credit.
restate_3: The math on this refinancing is simple: we were paying SORF plus 450 basis points on $575 million maturing in 1.9 years and we replaced that with $600 million at SORF plus 300 basis points with 6.2 years to maturity. That is 150 basis points of interest savings -- approximately $8.7 million per year in cash -- and a complete elimination of our near-term maturity risk. The 99.50 OID on the new facility is a modest cost. Harcastle structured the deal, Briarwood and Fenwick arranged, and we were 1.9 times oversubscribed at allocation from CLO managers and bank loan funds. Our 2,200-mile NGL pipeline and fractionation system in the Granville Basin generated $188 million of EBITDA in the twelve months through December 2025. The BB rating from Thornfield is consistent with where we have been, and the 5.5 times total leverage covenant gives us appropriate financial discipline. This was a clean and successful transaction that strengthens our long-term capital structure significantly.
restate_4: Midstream energy refinancings rarely make front pages, but Solvaren Energy Finance LLC's April 25 deal is a good example of smart liability management. The company swapped out a $575 million term loan bearing SORF plus 450 basis points and maturing in about two years for a new $600 million facility at SORF plus 300 basis points running for 6.2 years. Harcastle was the sole structuring bank and administrative agent, with Briarwood and Fenwick as joint lead arrangers. The book was 1.9 times covered by CLO managers and bank loan funds. Solvaren runs 2,200 miles of NGL pipeline and fractionation capacity in the Granville Basin and earned $188 million in EBITDA in 2025. paragraph: Westland Fiber Networks, Inc. completed the sale of $900 million aggregate principal amount of 5.750 percent senior secured first-lien notes due 2034 on February 10, 2026, in a private offering conducted under Rule 144A and Regulation S. Joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Ravensdale Capital priced the notes at 99.500 percent of par. Westland is a Noravia-based fiber-to-the-premises broadband provider serving approximately 1.9 million residential and 87,000 business subscribers across 41 metropolitan markets, with a total network footprint encompassing 28,400 route miles of fiber. The company reported revenues of $785 million and adjusted EBITDA of $348 million for fiscal year 2025, implying a pro forma net leverage ratio of approximately 4.0 times following the offering. Proceeds of approximately $890 million will be used to fund the Westland Accelerate 2028 network densification and footprint expansion program, targeting 650,000 incremental premises passed at an estimated capital intensity of $980 per home. The notes are secured on a first-lien basis by substantially all assets of Westland Fiber Networks and its material subsidiaries. Thornfield Ratings Services assigned a provisional BB rating to the notes, while Meridian Credit Ratings assigned BB-. Westland is 62 percent owned by private equity firm Castlegate Infrastructure Partners Fund III.

paragraph: Brighthollow Consumer Goods Corp. priced a secondary offering of 7.5 million shares of common stock at $44.00 per share on April 28, 2026, for gross proceeds of $330 million. The offering was structured entirely as a secondary sale by selling shareholder Drayfield Capital Partners Fund V, with the company receiving no proceeds. Sole book-runner Fenwick Meridian Group priced the offering at a 5.2 percent discount to Brighthollow's prior-day close of $46.41 on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under ticker BHCG. Drayfield Capital Partners Fund V's ownership position was reduced from 27.3 percent to 16.8 percent of outstanding shares following the offering. Brighthollow manufactures and markets premium pet nutrition products, home fragrance goods, and artisanal snack foods under 11 proprietary brands distributed through 62,000 retail locations and its own direct-to-consumer e-commerce platforms. The company reported net revenues of $1.18 billion for fiscal year 2025, with adjusted EBITDA of $212 million, representing an 18.0 percent margin. Brighthollow also announced separately that its board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million over the 12 months following the secondary offering, which management indicated will be funded from free cash flow. Remaining insiders are subject to a 60-day post-offering lock-up.

Drayfield's stake fell from 27.3 percent to 16.8 percent. paragraph: Paragon Cellular Holdings, Inc. completed the pricing of a $2.5 billion dual-tranche senior unsecured notes offering on March 26, 2026, consisting of $1.25 billion aggregate principal amount of 5.100 percent notes due 2031 and $1.25 billion aggregate principal amount of 5.600 percent notes due 2036. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Harcastle Investment Bank, Strathmore Capital Markets, Dunmore Equity Partners, and Pelletier & Wynne Securities. The 2031 notes were priced at 99.875 percent of par to yield 5.125 percent, and the 2036 notes were priced at 99.625 percent of par to yield 5.637 percent, representing spreads of 127 and 165 basis points, respectively, over comparable benchmark treasury securities. Total orders exceeded $7.8 billion across both tranches. Paragon operates a wireless telecommunications network serving approximately 48 million subscribers in 14 sovereign jurisdictions under the Velantian Economic Cooperative framework. Proceeds of approximately $2.47 billion will be used to refinance $1.9 billion of notes maturing in 2027 and 2028, fund spectrum license acquisitions announced in February 2026, and for general corporate purposes. Paragon Cellular Holdings carries an investment-grade rating of Baa2/BBB from Thornfield Ratings Services and Meridian Credit Ratings, respectively.
restate_0: Paragon Cellular Holdings completed a $2.5 billion two-tranche bond offering Thursday, selling $1.25 billion of 5.100 percent notes due 2031 and another $1.25 billion of 5.600 percent notes due 2036. Harcastle Investment Bank, Strathmore Capital Markets, Dunmore Equity Partners, and Pelletier & Wynne Securities co-led the deal. The 2031 tranche priced at 99.875 to yield 5.125 percent -- 127 basis points over treasuries. The 2036 tranche priced at 99.625 to yield 5.637 percent -- 165 basis points over treasuries. The combined order book exceeded $7.8 billion. Net proceeds of about $2.47 billion retire $1.9 billion of notes maturing in 2027 and 2028, fund spectrum acquisitions announced in February, and cover general corporate purposes. Paragon serves roughly 48 million wireless subscribers across 14 sovereign jurisdictions in the Velantian Economic Cooperative zone and carries investment-grade ratings of Baa2 from Thornfield and BBB from Meridian.
restate_1: This disclosure summarizes the dual-tranche senior unsecured note offering by Paragon Cellular Holdings, Inc. completed on March 26, 2026. The 2031 notes were priced at 99.875 percent of par to yield 5.125 percent, a spread of 127 basis points over the comparable benchmark. The 2036 notes were priced at 99.625 percent of par to yield 5.637 percent, a spread of 165 basis points over the comparable benchmark. Joint book-running managers were Harcastle Investment Bank, Strathmore Capital Markets, Dunmore Equity Partners, and Pelletier & Wynne Securities. Total orders across both tranches exceeded $7.8 billion. Net proceeds of approximately $2.47 billion will be used to refinance $1.9 billion of notes maturing in 2027 and 2028, fund spectrum license acquisitions announced in February 2026, and for general corporate purposes. Paragon Cellular Holdings carries an investment-grade rating of Baa2 from Thornfield Ratings Services and BBB from Meridian Credit Ratings.
restate_2: Paragon Cellular Holdings' $2.5 billion dual-tranche investment-grade offering is a landmark liability management and growth financing transaction. The 127 and 165 basis point spreads for the 2031 and 2036 tranches, respectively, are tight relative to recent Baa2/BBB-rated telecom issuance, reflecting the strength of Paragon's 48-million subscriber base and the stability of the Velantian Economic Cooperative regulatory framework. The $7.8 billion order book -- 3.1 times covered across $2.5 billion of supply -- demonstrates exceptional institutional demand. The use of $1.9 billion of proceeds to refinance notes maturing in 2027 and 2028 eliminates near-term maturity risk entirely, while the spectrum license acquisition funding positions Paragon for the next-generation network upgrade cycle. Four joint book-running managers -- Harcastle, Strathmore, Dunmore, and Pelletier -- indicate the scale and institutional breadth required for a deal of this size. We recommend Paragon's paper to investment-grade fixed-income portfolios as a core telecom infrastructure holding.
restate_3: $7.8 billion in orders for $2.5 billion in notes is an extraordinary result that reflects how institutional investors view Paragon's credit quality and strategic positioning. We serve approximately 48 million wireless subscribers across 14 sovereign jurisdictions in the Velantian Economic Cooperative, and our Baa2/BBB investment-grade ratings from Thornfield and Meridian reflect the consistency and durability of that revenue base. The two-tranche structure made sense: $1.25 billion of 5.100 percent notes due 2031 at 127 basis points over treasuries, and $1.25 billion of 5.600 percent notes due 2036 at 165 basis points over treasuries. Net proceeds of approximately $2.47 billion address our 2027 and 2028 maturities and fund the spectrum license acquisitions we announced in February. Harcastle, Strathmore, Dunmore, and Pelletier ran an impeccably coordinated syndication process. This transaction demonstrates the depth of our access to the global capital markets and ensures our financial flexibility for years to come.
restate_4: Telecom companies need to refinance debt regularly, and Paragon Cellular Holdings did it on a grand scale in March. The company priced a $2.5 billion two-part bond deal -- $1.25 billion of 5.100 percent notes due 2031 and $1.25 billion of 5.600 percent notes due 2036. The 2031 tranche came at 99.875 cents, yielding 5.125 percent, or 127 basis points over comparable treasuries. The 2036 tranche priced at 99.625 cents to yield 5.637 percent, or 165 basis points over. The combined order book hit $7.8 billion -- more than three times the deal size. Net proceeds of $2.47 billion pay off $1.9 billion of notes due in 2027 and 2028 and fund spectrum licenses announced earlier in the year. Paragon serves 48 million wireless subscribers across 14 countries under the Velantian Economic Cooperative. It is investment-grade rated -- Baa2 from Thornfield, BBB from Meridian -- which is why it can print at these spreads. Four major banks co-led the offering.

paragraph: Verindal Real Estate Finance, Inc. priced its initial public offering of mortgage investment trust shares on January 14, 2026, selling 24.0 million shares at $20.00 per share for gross proceeds of $480 million. Joint book-running managers Albrecht Whitmore Securities and Briarwood Securities led the offering. The company intends to invest its IPO proceeds, together with leverage from committed credit facilities totaling $850 million, to invest in a diversified portfolio of first-lien commercial real estate bridge loans, mezzanine debt instruments, and preferred equity interests collateralized by transitional income-producing properties. The target portfolio yield at inception is estimated at 9.8 percent on a blended basis, with a projected dividend yield of approximately 8.5 percent based on the offering price, payable quarterly. The overallotment option covering 3.6 million shares at $20.00 was exercised in full on January 18, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $552 million. Shares began trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under the ticker VRDF, opening at $20.85 and closing at $20.50 on the first day of trading. The company's external manager, Verindal Capital Management LLC, will receive an annualized base management fee of 1.50 percent of equity and a 20 percent incentive fee on returns above an 8.0 percent hurdle. Officers and directors are subject to a 180-day lock-up restriction.

paragraph: Stonegate Payments Infrastructure Corp. priced $450 million aggregate principal amount of 4.875 percent senior notes due 2030 on May 1, 2026, in a public offering registered under the Centralia Securities Disclosure Ordinance. The notes were issued at 99.750 percent of par through joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Fenwick Meridian Group, with Ravensdale Capital as co-manager. The offering attracted final demand of approximately $1.6 billion from a geographically diversified investor base. Stonegate operates payment settlement rails, card-switch infrastructure, and ATM network technology serving 1,470 member financial institutions across the Centralia monetary union. The company reported revenues of $562 million for fiscal year 2025, with adjusted EBITDA of $247 million and free cash flow conversion of 88 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $440 million will refinance $395 million of outstanding floating-rate term borrowings and fund $45 million of capital expenditures associated with next-generation ISO 20022-compliant settlement architecture. The notes were assigned an investment-grade rating of Baa3 by Thornfield Ratings Services and BBB- by Meridian Credit Ratings. Stonegate is majority-owned by a consortium of member financial institutions and a 28 percent strategic stake held by Castlegate Infrastructure Partners. Annual interest savings from the transaction are estimated at approximately $6.2 million compared to the replaced floating-rate debt.

restate_2: Stonegate Payments Infrastructure Corp.'s $450 million investment-grade note issuance is a straightforward liability management and growth capex financing. Replacing $395 million of floating-rate term debt with $440 million of fixed-rate notes at 4.875 percent locks in $6.2 million of annual interest savings while funding $45 million of ISO 20022 compliance infrastructure -- a necessary investment that also serves as a competitive moat against non-compliant alternative payment providers. With 88 percent free cash flow conversion on $247 million of adjusted EBITDA from $562 million of revenues, Stonegate has the cash flow capacity to service fixed obligations comfortably. The Baa3/BBB- investment-grade ratings from Thornfield and Meridian reflect the quasi-utility nature of the network serving 1,470 member institutions across the Centralia monetary union. The member institution ownership consortium and 28 percent Castlegate Infrastructure Partners stake provide strong governance alignment. The $1.6 billion of orders -- 3.6 times the deal size -- validates the credit quality and pricing.
restate_3: The ISO 20022 migration is not optional for payment infrastructure operators -- it is a regulatory requirement that our 1,470 member institutions are depending on us to execute on time. The $45 million of capex funded through this offering goes directly to that next-generation settlement architecture, and it is exactly the kind of investment that deepens the value of our network for members. The $395 million of floating-rate term debt we are retiring simultaneously saves us approximately $6.2 million per year in interest, which is meaningful. We priced $450 million of 4.875 percent senior notes at 99.750 under the Centralia Securities Disclosure Ordinance, with Garvey Lynch and Fenwick as joint book-runners and Ravensdale as co-manager. Final orders reached $1.6 billion, reflecting strong investor confidence in our business. Adjusted EBITDA of $247 million, revenue of $562 million, 88 percent free cash flow conversion -- those metrics speak to the quality of this franchise. Baa3 from Thornfield and BBB- from Meridian confirm our investment-grade standing. Castlegate holds 28 percent alongside our member institution consortium.
restate_4: Stonegate Payments Infrastructure Corp. is the kind of company that does not generate IPO headlines but sits at the center of a national payments ecosystem. The operator of settlement rails, card-switch infrastructure, and ATM networks for 1,470 financial institutions in the Centralia monetary union sold $450 million of 4.875 percent senior notes due 2030 at 99.750 cents on May 1, pulling in $1.6 billion in orders -- 3.6 times the deal size. Garvey Lynch and Fenwick Meridian led; Ravensdale was co-manager. Net proceeds of $440 million retire $395 million of floating-rate debt and fund $45 million of capex for ISO 20022-compliant settlement architecture. The interest savings compared to the floating-rate debt: about $6.2 million annually. Stonegate had $562 million in revenue, $247 million in adjusted EBITDA, and 88 percent free cash flow conversion in fiscal 2025. Thornfield rates the notes Baa3; Meridian rates them BBB- -- investment grade. Castlegate Infrastructure Partners holds a 28 percent strategic stake.

paragraph: Arvolex Biosystems Corp. priced an upsized follow-on offering of 5.5 million shares at $112.00 per share on February 23, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $616 million, including full exercise of the 825,000 share overallotment option. The offering comprised 4.0 million primary shares and 1.5 million secondary shares sold by pre-IPO institutional holders Cascadia Life Sciences Fund II and Goldstream Bioequity Partners. Net primary proceeds of approximately $434 million will fund Phase III clinical trials of ARV-440, a gene-editing therapeutic targeting rare monogenic liver disorders, and to expand the company's proprietary protein-engineering research platform. Strathmore Capital Markets acted as sole book-running manager. The offering priced at a 4.1 percent premium to the prior closing price of $107.60 on the Aldermoor Exchange, reflecting significant inbound investor demand ahead of ARV-440 Phase II data readout scheduled for June 2026. Arvolex shares closed at $121.40 on the day of the offering announcement, up 12.8 percent. The company reported cash and equivalents of $287 million before the offering and estimated its cash runway extended to the first quarter of 2029 following completion of the transaction. Remaining insiders are restricted by a 60-day lock-up agreement. Cascadia Life Sciences Fund II reduced its ownership from 9.3 percent to 5.9 percent.

restate_2: Arvolex Biosystems' upsized follow-on at $112.00 -- a 4.1 percent premium to the prior close of $107.60 -- is one of the most constructive pricing signals we have seen in the gene editing therapeutic space and reinforces our Outperform rating. paragraph: Redstone Mobility Services, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 20.0 million shares at $14.00 per share on April 16, 2026, for gross proceeds of $280 million on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under ticker RMSV. Joint book-running managers Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Albrecht Whitmore Securities led the offering. The deal was 7.1 times oversubscribed and priced at the midpoint of the $13.00 to $15.00 preliminary range. Redstone operates an on-demand ride-hailing and micro-mobility sharing platform serving 38 cities in 12 countries, with 8.4 million monthly active riders and 1.6 million registered drivers and mobility operators as of December 31, 2025. The company reported net revenues of $498 million for fiscal year 2025, representing 31 percent growth, and generated adjusted EBITDA of $42 million, its first fiscal year of positive adjusted EBITDA. Net proceeds of approximately $257 million will fund market expansion into 14 additional cities, investment in electric vehicle fleet electrification incentive programs, and technology development including route-optimization AI models. The overallotment option covering 3.0 million additional shares at $14.00 was exercised in full on April 19, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $322 million. Insiders are subject to 180-day lock-ups expiring October 13, 2026.

paragraph: Lumivax Therapeutics, Inc. completed a $192 million overnight follow-on offering of 8.0 million shares of common stock at $24.00 per share on March 4, 2026, representing a 7.3 percent discount to the prior closing price of $25.89 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange. Sole book-runner Dunmore Equity Partners ran an accelerated overnight book-build that closed within six hours of launch. The offering size was increased from an originally contemplated $150 million due to demand from dedicated healthcare investors and multi-sector long-only funds. The company reported research collaboration revenues of $38 million for fiscal year 2025 and ended the year with cash and equivalents of $143 million. Net proceeds of approximately $183 million will extend the company's cash runway through mid-2029, fund the initiation of two additional Phase II studies, and support regulatory submission activities for LVX-201, its lead malaria vaccine candidate, in six endemic-country regulatory authorities. The overallotment option covering 1.2 million additional shares was exercised on March 7, 2026. Officers and directors are subject to 90-day lock-up restrictions.

Dunmore Equity Partners ran the sole book, selling 8.0 million shares at $24.00 each -- a 7.3 percent discount to the prior close of $25.89 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange. The deal grew from an originally planned $150 million as demand from healthcare specialists and long-only multi-sector funds exceeded expectations. The 1.2 million share greenshoe was exercised March 7. Lumivax is developing mRNA-based vaccines against neglected tropical diseases and emerging pathogens, with three Phase II candidates and one in Phase I. Research collaboration revenues were $38 million in fiscal 2025 and year-end cash was $143 million. Net proceeds of about $183 million extend the cash runway to mid-2029 and fund two new Phase II studies plus regulatory submissions for LVX-201, the lead malaria vaccine, in six endemic-country regulators. Officers and directors are locked for 90 days.

paragraph: Silvermoor Renewable Power Finance Ltd. priced €600 million aggregate principal amount of 4.250 percent green notes due 2035 on January 20, 2026, in a Regulation S-only offering targeted at European institutional investors. Joint book-running managers Harcastle Investment Bank and Strathmore Capital Markets led the transaction, which attracted orders exceeding €2.9 billion from more than 220 accounts, including sovereign wealth funds, insurance general accounts, and dedicated green bond mandates. The notes were priced at 99.700 percent of par, representing a spread of 165 basis points over the comparable Noravia government benchmark. Silvermoor is a holding company for a portfolio of 34 operational solar and onshore wind projects totaling 3,850 megawatts of installed capacity across six Valorian Economic Zone members. The company reported revenues of €482 million and adjusted EBITDA of €378 million for fiscal year 2025, with a weighted average power purchase agreement tenor of 11.3 remaining years. Proceeds of approximately €594 million will be applied to refinance €450 million of senior secured notes due 2027, fund €100 million of discretionary capital expenditures, and pay transaction costs. The notes received a BBB- rating from Meridian Credit Ratings and a Baa3 rating from Thornfield Ratings Services, placing Silvermoor firmly within the investment-grade universe for the first time following a two-notch upgrade in October 2025.

Notably, Silvermoor is investment grade for the first time after a two-notch rating upgrade in October 2025 -- BBB- from Meridian and Baa3 from Thornfield.

paragraph: Kestral Consumer Technology, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 28.0 million shares of common stock at $13.00 per share on February 6, 2026, for gross proceeds of $364 million. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Fenwick Meridian Group. An overallotment option covering 4.2 million shares was partially exercised, with underwriters purchasing 3.5 million shares for additional gross proceeds of $45.5 million, bringing total gross proceeds to $409.5 million. Kestral designs and sells smart home security systems, AI-enabled door locks, and connected environmental sensors through retail channels and a direct subscription security monitoring service. The company reported revenues of $519 million for fiscal year 2025, with 71 percent recurring revenue from monitoring and software subscriptions and a net dollar retention rate of 108 percent. Kestral reported adjusted EBITDA of $78 million for fiscal year 2025, representing a 15.0 percent margin. Net proceeds of approximately $375 million will fund retail expansion into mass-market channels, subsidize hardware bundling with multi-year monitoring contracts, and invest in AI-native threat detection software capabilities. Shares closed at $15.70 on the first trading day on the Aldermoor Exchange under ticker KSRL, a 20.8 percent first-day gain. All insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions expiring August 5, 2026.

restate_2: Kestral Consumer Technology enters coverage at Outperform, $21 price target. The IPO at $13.00 -- total proceeds $409.5 million including partial greenshoe -- gives a company with strong recurring revenue fundamentals a meaningful capital base to accelerate growth. The 71 percent recurring revenue mix from monitoring and software subscriptions on $519 million of total fiscal 2025 revenue is a hardware-plus-recurring-revenue model that resembles ADT's evolution toward connected security, but with meaningfully higher software content. The 108 percent net dollar retention -- implying existing customers are expanding their spend -- is a durable growth indicator. paragraph: Torrel Specialty Finance Corp. completed the offering of $325 million aggregate principal amount of 6.750 percent senior unsecured notes due 2029 on March 13, 2026, in a private placement to qualified institutional buyers pursuant to Rule 144A. paragraph: Meridian Ports Authority Trust completed a $700 million infrastructure bond offering on April 5, 2026, issuing 4.625 percent revenue bonds due 2046 in a public transaction registered under the Valorian Municipal Securities Transparency Ordinance. Albrecht Whitmore Securities and Ravensdale Capital served as joint senior managers, with Pelletier & Wynne Securities as co-senior manager. The bonds were priced at 99.250 percent of par to yield 4.665 percent, representing a spread of 142 basis points over the 20-year benchmark Valorian sovereign bond yield of 3.245 percent. The offering attracted final orders from 104 institutional investors including pension funds, insurance companies, and infrastructure-focused closed-end funds. Meridian Ports Authority Trust is the statutory financing authority for the Meridian Metropolitan Port Complex, the largest containerized cargo facility in the Central Valorian coastal region, handling 6.4 million twenty-foot equivalent units annually and generating operating revenues of $498 million for fiscal year 2025. Bond proceeds of approximately $692 million will fund Phase III of the port's capacity expansion and modernization program, encompassing construction of two additional deep-water berths capable of accommodating ultra-large container vessels and installation of 18 electrified automated stacking cranes. The bonds are secured by a pledge of port operating revenues and carry an AA rating from Meridian Credit Ratings.

The bonds are secured by port operating revenues and carry a AA rating from Meridian Credit Ratings.

paragraph: Halcyon Materials Science, Inc. priced a $250 million aggregate principal amount of 1.750 percent convertible senior notes due 2030 on January 31, 2026, in a Rule 144A private placement. Joint book-running managers Strathmore Capital Markets and Dunmore Equity Partners led the offering. The notes were priced at par with an initial conversion premium of 35 percent above the reference price of $67.40 per Halcyon share, establishing an initial conversion price of approximately $91.00. The initial purchasers were also granted an option to purchase up to $37.5 million in additional principal amount of notes, which was exercised in full, bringing total proceeds to $287.5 million. Halcyon intends to use net proceeds of approximately $281 million to fund the development and scale-up of its proprietary solid-state electrolyte manufacturing process, including construction of a 200,000 square foot pilot-to-production facility at the Greenbrooke Technology Park. The company simultaneously entered into capped call transactions with a cap price of approximately $134.80 per share, representing a 100 percent premium to the reference share price. Halcyon reported revenues of $94 million for fiscal year 2025, primarily from government-sponsored research contracts, with total liquidity of $178 million prior to the offering. Shares of Halcyon trade on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker HLCY.

To reduce dilution risk at conversion, Halcyon simultaneously purchased capped calls with a cap at approximately $134.80 per share -- double the reference price. Net proceeds of about $281 million fund development and scale-up of the company's solid-state electrolyte manufacturing process, including building a 200,000 square foot pilot-to-production facility at the Greenbrooke Technology Park. The company had $94 million in revenue in fiscal 2025, mostly from government research contracts, and $178 million of pre-offering liquidity. HLCY trades on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange.

Simultaneously with the offering, Halcyon entered into capped call transactions with a cap price of approximately $134.80 per share, a 100 percent premium to the reference price. restate_2: Halcyon Materials Science's $287.5 million convertible note raise is a well-structured financing for advancing solid-state electrolyte manufacturing toward commercial scale. The 35 percent conversion premium at $91.00 over the $67.40 reference price -- paired with capped calls at $134.80 -- effectively creates a range in which the company benefits from equity appreciation while limiting dilution. restate_3: The convertible note structure was the ideal choice for Halcyon at this stage. paragraph: Granwood Residential Communities, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 15.0 million shares of common stock at $22.00 per share on April 24, 2026, for gross proceeds of $330 million. Joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Harcastle Investment Bank led the transaction. Granwood owns and operates a portfolio of 47 single-family rental communities comprising 6,840 homes across 11 Sunbelt and Mountain West submarkets in the Valorian Federal territories, targeting workforce-demographic residents with median household incomes between $65,000 and $110,000 annually. The company reported total revenues of $387 million, same-community net operating income growth of 7.2 percent, and adjusted funds from operations of $1.38 per diluted share for fiscal year 2025, with a year-end portfolio occupancy rate of 96.4 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $303 million will fund the acquisition and development of approximately 1,200 additional homes currently under contract or in pre-development. The overallotment option covering 2.25 million shares was exercised in full on April 28, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $379.5 million. Shares began trading on the Aldermoor Exchange under ticker GRWC, opening at $25.50 and closing at $24.10, a 9.5 percent first-day gain. All insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions expiring October 21, 2026.

paragraph: Portsmith Logistics Corp. raised $1.0 billion through a high-yield bond offering on March 28, 2026, issuing $600 million aggregate principal amount of 8.000 percent first-lien senior secured notes due 2031 and $400 million aggregate principal amount of 10.250 percent second-lien senior secured notes due 2032. Joint book-running managers Briarwood Securities and Fenwick Meridian Group priced the first-lien notes at 99.00 percent and the second-lien notes at 98.00 percent of par. Portsmith is a provider of contract logistics, warehousing, and last-mile delivery services with 108 distribution centers aggregating 38 million square feet of warehouse space across the Valorian and Noravia freight networks. The company was acquired by private equity sponsor Talbourne Capital Partners in a leveraged transaction in 2023 for a total enterprise value of $2.8 billion. Portsmith reported revenues of $3.1 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $275 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025. Pro forma total debt following the offering is approximately $1.09 billion, implying total net leverage of 3.78 times EBITDA. Proceeds will be used to refinance existing credit facilities, pay transaction fees and expenses of approximately $42 million, and distribute $148 million to equity holders. The first-lien notes were rated B+ by Thornfield Ratings Services; the second-lien notes were rated B-.

restate_2: Portsmith Logistics Corp.'s $1.0 billion dual-tranche high-yield offering is a recapitalization that serves Talbourne Capital Partners' portfolio management while maintaining manageable post-transaction leverage. The company sold $600 million of 8.000 percent first-lien notes due 2031 at 99.00 cents and $400 million of 10.250 percent second-lien notes due 2032 at 98.00 cents -- $1.0 billion total -- with Briarwood Securities and Fenwick Meridian Group running both tranches. Portsmith runs 108 distribution centers with 38 million square feet of warehouse space across the Valorian and Noravia freight networks. Talbourne Capital Partners bought the business in 2023 for $2.8 billion. Revenue was $3.1 billion and adjusted EBITDA was $275 million for fiscal 2025. After the offering, total debt is about $1.09 billion and net leverage is 3.78 times. Proceeds refinance old debt, pay $42 million in fees, and return $148 million to equity holders. Thornfield rates the first-lien B+ and the second-lien B-.

paragraph: Orenthal Advanced Diagnostics, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 9.0 million shares at $29.00 per share on February 25, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $261 million. Sole book-runner Pelletier & Wynne Securities priced the offering at the top of the $26.00 to $29.00 preliminary range following an oversubscribed roadshow. The overallotment option covering 1.35 million shares was exercised in full on March 1, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $300.15 million. Orenthal develops point-of-care molecular diagnostic platforms using proprietary microfluidic cartridge technology capable of delivering multiplex pathogen identification results within 22 minutes in decentralized care settings. The company's installed base comprised 8,700 instruments across 54 countries as of December 31, 2025, with consumable revenue of $178 million and instrument revenue of $64 million in fiscal year 2025. Net proceeds of approximately $275 million will fund commercialization of two additional instrument platforms currently in pre-commercial production, expand the international field service organization by 230 positions, and fund performance of a post-market clinical registry study required by regulators in four jurisdictions. Shares opened at $40.00 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker ORND and closed at $37.50, a 29.3 percent first-day gain. All insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions.

paragraph: Tavenridge Grid Technologies, Inc. completed an underwritten offering of 6.5 million shares of common stock at $55.00 per share on May 7, 2026, for gross proceeds of $357.5 million. The offering comprised 5.0 million newly issued shares from the company and 1.5 million secondary shares sold by pre-IPO shareholder Irongate Ventures Fund III. Sole book-runner Strathmore Capital Markets led the offering, which priced at a 3.9 percent discount to the prior-day closing price of $57.24 on the Aldermoor Exchange. Net primary proceeds of approximately $267 million will fund an accelerated production ramp-up of Tavenridge's grid-scale solid-state battery storage systems and the establishment of a second manufacturing facility at the Aldervein Industrial Campus, with an expected annual production capacity of 4.0 gigawatt-hours upon full commissioning in the fourth quarter of 2027. Tavenridge reported revenues of $318 million for fiscal year 2025, including $211 million from utility-scale storage system deliveries and $107 million from grid-integration software licensing. The company reported a backlog of $1.64 billion as of March 31, 2026. Irongate Ventures Fund III's ownership stake declined from 11.8 percent to 8.6 percent following the offering. Remaining officers and directors are subject to a 90-day lock-up restriction expiring August 5, 2026.

Tavenridge had $318 million in 2025 revenues -- $211 million from utility-scale storage deliveries and $107 million from grid-integration software licensing -- and a backlog of $1.64 billion as of March 31. Officers and directors face a 90-day lockup through August 5.

paragraph: Delvecchio Aerospace Systems Corp. priced its initial public offering of 19.5 million shares of common stock at $21.00 per share on March 20, 2026, for gross proceeds of $409.5 million. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co., Harcastle Investment Bank, and Albrecht Whitmore Securities. The transaction was 12.3 times oversubscribed, with final orders received from 310 institutional accounts including a significant allocation to sovereign wealth funds. The IPO priced above the $18.00 to $20.00 per share preliminary range established during the international roadshow. Delvecchio manufactures satellite bus platforms, payload integration systems, and launch vehicle adapters for commercial, government, and scientific satellite customers, with a contract backlog of $2.9 billion as of December 31, 2025. The company reported revenues of $763 million for fiscal year 2025, growing 28 percent year-over-year, and adjusted EBITDA of $148 million, a 19.4 percent margin. Net proceeds of approximately $376 million will fund the construction of a new low-earth-orbit satellite manufacturing line with annual throughput capacity of 120 bus platforms, invest in proprietary electric propulsion development, and retire $85 million of mezzanine debt. Shares opened at $30.50 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker DVCA, closing at $28.90 on the first trading day, a 37.6 percent gain. Insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions expiring September 16, 2026.
